'''Muzzle''' was a [[Genetically defective clone|defective]] [[clone trooper]] who served as an [[Advanced Recon Commando]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. A [[human]] [[Cloning|clone]], Muzzle was created on the [[planet]] [[Kamino]] from the [[Clone template|genetic template]] of [[bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett]],<ref name="Collapse" /> and as such had [[Color|tan]] skin, a height of 1.83 [[meter]]s (6 [[Foot|ft]]),<ref name="Clones" /> and a weight of 80 [[kilogram]]s.<ref name="Weight" /> They were also implanted with a [[behavioral modification biochip]] by the [[Kaminoan]] [[Scientist|scientists]] to ensure that they would not resist certain [[Executive Order Codes for the Grand Army of the Republic|orders]].<ref name="Clones" /> Muzzle eventually [[Desertion|deserted]] the Republic and led the [[mercenary]] organization [[Aurodium Sword]], which typically favored jobs for providing [[Bodyguard|security]] to Galactic Republic corporate personnel, such as [[Raith Sienar]].<ref name="Collapse" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:A-66.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Muzzle's ''Star Wars'' Legends depiction]]
Muzzle was mentioned in the current ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] in ''[[Collapse of the Republic]]'', a [[2019]] supplement to [[Fantasy Flight Games]]' series of ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying]]'' products.<ref name="Collapse" /> Muzzle was first mentioned in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]], where the clone was identified as [[Muzzle/Legends|Alpha-66]] in "[[Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy]]," an article authored by [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Ryan Kaufman (real person)|Ryan Kaufman]] for the [[Star Wars Insider 89|eighty-ninth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazines,<ref name="Underworld">{{InsiderCite|89|Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy}}</ref> released on [[August 9]], [[2006]].<ref name="InsiderDate">[[Star Wars Insider 88|''Star Wars Insider'' 88]]</ref> In the article, Muzzle's depiction was created by illustrator [[Joe Corroney]].<ref name="Underworld" />
